picks.rationale: Spain's defensive record in the tournament is exceptional, having conceded only one goal across six matches and keeping six consecutive clean sheets. This defensive solidity, coupled with the typically cautious nature of a World Cup semi-final, strongly suggests a low-scoring affair. Historically, Spain's World Cup knockout matches since 2010 have seen under 2.5 goals in seven out of nine instances. Both teams will prioritize not conceding, making goals a premium in this high-stakes encounter.

France enters this semi-final with a perfect 6-0 record in the tournament, showcasing exceptional form and winning all their matches. Their attack, spearheaded by Kylian Mbappé, who has 8 goals, has scored 16 goals in six matches. While Spain's defense is formidable, France's ability to create and convert chances, even against elite defenses, gives them an edge. France is expected to field a strong lineup with Mbappé and Tchouaméni.

Spain boasts an impressive national-record 36-game unbeaten streak and has conceded only one goal throughout the current World Cup campaign. They have also demonstrated a knack for winning against France in recent high-stakes encounters, including the 2025 Nations League semi-final (5-4) and the 2024 European Championship semi-final (2-1). The significant line movement towards Spain from an opening of +380 to the current +220 suggests increased market confidence in their ability to pull off the upset. Their defensive resilience and recent head-to-head success against France make Spain at +220 a compelling high-confidence pick.

Despite both teams' strong defenses, recent head-to-head matchups between Spain and France have been surprisingly high-scoring, with the last two semi-finals seeing 5-4 and 2-1 scorelines. France's potent attack has netted 16 goals in six matches, while Spain has scored 11. Key offensive players like Kylian Mbappé (8 goals) and Ousmane Dembélé (5 goals) for France, along with Spain's Lamine Yamal and Mikel Oyarzabal, possess the quality to break down even the best defenses. This offensive firepower could lead to a more open game than anticipated for a semi-final.

France has demonstrated formidable defensive capabilities, recording four consecutive clean sheets in the knockout rounds of this tournament and conceding only two goals overall. While both teams possess potent attacks, their defensive discipline and the immense pressure of a World Cup semi-final are likely to lead to a tightly contested match with limited scoring opportunities. The expected return of Aurélien Tchouaméni to France's midfield will further bolster their defensive balance. This tactical battle is set to be a low-scoring affair.
